entities.name: PIGEON PEA
entities.description: A legume (Cajanus cajan) production of which is constrained by sterility mosaic disease in the Indian subcontinent

entities.name: STERILITY MOSAIC DISEASE
entities.description: A disease that is a major constraint on pigeon pea production, characterized by sterility

entities.name: INDIAN SUBCONTINENT
entities.description: The geographic region where sterility mosaic disease is a major constraint on pigeon pea production

entities.name: ACERIA CAJANI
entities.description: An eriophyid mite that transmits the agent of sterility mosaic disease

entities.name: VIRUS-LIKE PARTICLES
entities.description: Highly flexuous filamentous particles of 3 to 10 nm in width isolated from SMD-affected pigeon pea plants

entities.name: NICOTIANA BENTHAMIANA
entities.description: A plant species used to purify virus-like particle preparations

entities.name: RT-PCR
entities.description: Reverse transcriptase-polymerase chain reaction used to amplify a 321 bp product specifically from SMD-affected pigeon pea plants

entities.name: ELISA
entities.description: Enzyme-linked immunosorbent assay used to detect the virus in infected plants

entities.name: WESTERN IMMUNOBLOTTING
entities.description: A technique used to detect the virus-specific 32-kDa protein in infected plants

entities.name: INDIA
entities.description: The country where SMD-affected pigeon pea plant samples were collected and tested

entities.name: PIGEON PEA STERILITY MOSAIC VIRUS
entities.description: A novel virus (PPSMV) identified as the agent of sterility mosaic disease in pigeon pea

entities.name: TOSPOVIRUS
entities.description: A genus of viruses with properties similar to PPSMV

entities.name: TENUIVIRUS
entities.description: A genus of viruses with properties similar to PPSMV

entities.name: HIGH PLAINS VIRUS
entities.description: An eriophyid mite-transmitted virus (HPV) with properties similar to PPSMV

entities.name: GRAFT-INOCULATION
entities.description: A method of transmitting the virus using SMD-affected tissue to infect healthy pigeon pea plants
